title: Nephrodium deltoideum
intro: Nephrodium deltoideum, also known as Lady fern or Delta fern, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Blechnaceae, commonly found in shaded, moist environments. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Nephrodium deltoideum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Nephrodium deltoideum is often prepared as a decoction or infused as a tea to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in some traditional remedies for its potential antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects.
taxonomy: Nephrodium deltoideum is classified within the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is a type of fern that belongs to the class Polypodiopsida. The genus Nephrodium is now considered a synonym of the genus Dryopteris, and therefore, Nephrodium deltoideum is often referred to as Dryopteris deltoidea. This classification change is a result of ongoing revisions in the field of fern taxonomy. As a result, the classification of Nephrodium deltoideum has been updated to reflect its new position within the Dryopteris genus.
